_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Congenital Heart Disease (D-TGA, large VSD, pulmonary stenosis)
clinical_indications: Patients presenting with D-TGA, a large VSD, and pulmonary stenosis.

clinical_thresholds.value: D-TGA, a large VSD, and pulmonary stenosis

claim.label: Post procedure stills of ECHO with report

ppd_preauth.q: Do the clinical notes detail history, signs & symptoms, and indication for procedure?
Was the Echo/ Doppler report suggestive of D-TGA, a large VSD, and pulmonary stenosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the indoor case papers submitted?
Are the detailed Procedure / Operative notes submitted?
Does the Post procedure still of ECHO show repair of the defect?
Is there a Detailed Discharge Summary mentioning date of follow-up submitted?

common_queries: Echo/Doppler report is not suggestive of D-TGA, a large VSD, and pulmonary stenosis, or the report is missing.
Clinical notes are missing or do not detail history, signs & symptoms, and indication for the procedure.
Post-procedure ECHO stills do not show repair of the defect, or are missing.
Detailed Discharge Summary is missing or does not mention the date of follow-up.
Indoor case papers or Procedure/Operative notes are missing.

clinical_key_pointers: The Rastelli procedure is indicated in patients presenting with D-TGA, a large VSD, and pulmonary stenosis. During this procedure, the VSD is closed using a baffle. By doing so, oxygenated blood from the left ventricle is directed into the aorta. A conduit is then placed from the right ventricle to the pulmonary artery thus shunting deoxygenated blood into the pulmonary artery.

The Rastelli repair requires cardiopulmonary bypass and aortic cross-clamping. The ventricular septal defect is visualized through a right ventriculotomy. Obstructive right ventricular muscle is excised, and a large intra-ventricular baffle is sutured into place closing the ventricular septal defect and redirecting left ventricular outflow to the more anteriorly placed aortic valve. A valved homograft conduit is utilized to achieve right ventricular to pulmonary artery continuity. Transesophageal echocardiography is utilized to help assess adequacy of repair. Cardiopulmonary bypass time and aortic cross-clamp time required to complete repair is usually moderate to long.
